questions.text: It is a rainy Sunday in the Gryffindor common room and someone wonders aloud how to pass the long afternoon.

options.label: You reel off half a dozen schemes before the fire has finished crackling
You offer a couple of genuinely fun ideas worth trying
You have one idea, and you share it
You wait to hear what everyone else fancies doing
You have nothing; you would rather just follow along

questions.text: You step into an abandoned classroom full of dusty, forgotten magical objects and are asked what could be made of it.

options.label: You picture the whole place transformed, every cobwebbed corner reborn
You imagine it clearly enough to describe it
You see roughly what it might become
You struggle to picture much beyond the dust
You see only an empty room; imagining more is a waste of time

questions.text: A professor lays out the tangled theory behind a fiendishly complicated new spell.

options.label: You have it worked out before they finish the explanation
You follow it easily
You get there after a moment's thought
You need it explained a second time
You give up; that kind of theory just loses you

questions.text: Late by the fire the talk drifts to deep questions of magic, fate, and what lies beyond the Veil.

options.label: That kind of talk bores you; you would rather raid the kitchens
You have little patience for it, but you sit through it
You will join in if it turns to something practical
You find the ideas worth chewing on
You love wondering about the great mysteries for their own sake

questions.text: A friend is cornered and needs a clever, made-up excuse to explain why they were out of bounds after curfew.

options.label: Nothing comes to you; inventing stories just is not in you
You manage little; that kind of inventiveness escapes you
You can force out one plain, obvious excuse
A decent cover story takes shape after some thought
Three ridiculous alibis spring to mind at once

questions.text: A crucial exam in a difficult subject is only a week away.

options.label: A colour-coded revision timetable, every topic timed to the minute
A solid study plan made and the schedule set
A rough plan, good enough to start revising
You will cram it the night before
You had not really thought about it

questions.text: You are brewing a tricky potion from a long, exacting set of instructions.

options.label: You catch every step and the tiny warnings buried in the footnotes
You read it closely and follow the key steps
You skim it for the main points
You trust it will probably turn out fine
You just chuck the ingredients in and hope

questions.text: Your trunk, your desk, and the state of your corner of the dormitory are entirely up to you.

options.label: Everything labelled and in its place, quills sorted just so
You keep it mostly tidy and your things in order
Neat where it matters, chaos everywhere else
You let it pile up and sort it out later
A glorious mess suits you fine; tidying is overrated

questions.text: You are put in charge of the supplies and schedule for a secret study group.

options.label: Notes lost and meetings double-booked; it falls apart within a week
Things slip and get muddled more often than not
A few mistakes creep in, nothing serious
It mostly stays in order
You run it flawlessly, every detail accounted for

questions.text: It is your turn for the dull duty nobody wants, like a late-night round of prefect patrols or scrubbing cauldrons in detention.

options.label: You talk someone else into covering it; it can wait
You put it off whenever you can
You do it, grumbling and late
You get it done without complaint
Your turn is your turn; you would never shirk it

questions.text: The Great Hall is roaring with a victory feast after a Quidditch final, candles blazing overhead.

options.label: You are the centre of it, leading the cheers and the songs
You are lively and pull people in around you
You enjoy yourself with a few good friends
You hang near the edges of the hall
You slip off early; crowds like this wear you out

questions.text: It is the first night on the Hogwarts Express and your compartment fills with witches and wizards you have never met.

options.label: You are introducing yourself before the trolley reaches your door
You happily strike up talk with whoever is nearby
You will say something if there is a good reason
You wait for someone to speak to you first
You say nothing unless you absolutely have to

questions.text: The common room falls quiet and everyone turns to hear you recount the adventure you have just survived.

options.label: You love every eye on you; the floor is yours
You are comfortable holding the room
You manage it well enough
You would rather the attention move on quickly
You freeze up and wish someone else would tell it

questions.text: The whole house is crammed into the common room, all talking over each other after a long day of lessons.

options.label: You sit back and watch, taking it all in, saying little
You hang back more often than not
You jump in only when you have something to add
You make yourself heard in the mix
You put yourself right at the centre of it all

questions.text: You are seated at the feast beside an exchange student from a faraway wizarding school you have never met.

options.label: You stay quiet and let the silence sit
You speak only when spoken to
You offer a few careful words
You chat with them easily enough
You are talking like old friends within minutes

questions.text: A friend slumps down beside you, crushed after a cruel letter from home.

options.label: You feel their heartache as your own and stay with them for hours
You are moved, and you offer real comfort
You acknowledge it and try to move them along
You notice, but feel little yourself
You tell them to get over it already

questions.text: A classmate begs for your help with a mountain of homework on the one free afternoon you had all week.

options.label: You drop your plans entirely and help them all afternoon
You make the time to lend a hand
You swing by to help for a little while
You suggest they ask someone else
You wave it off; you had plans of your own

questions.text: A nervous first-year stands alone at the edge of the room, too shy to say a word to anyone.

options.label: You go out of your way to make them feel welcome
You are warm and friendly toward them
You are polite enough
You ignore how awkward they feel
You let them squirm

questions.text: A rival takes a sly, sneering dig at you in front of a crowd in the corridor, fishing for a reaction.

options.label: You fire back with the most cutting line you have
You return the insult sharply
You give a measured comeback
You let it slide without a word
You answer the sneer with plain kindness

questions.text: You arrive at a gathering of wizards full of unfamiliar faces and names.

options.label: Other people's lives do not interest you in the least
You have little curiosity about any of them
You will listen if it happens to involve you
You are fairly curious about the people here
You want to know everyone's whole story before the night is out

questions.text: Two disasters strike at once: a spell has gone wrong in your hands and a professor is bearing down on you.

options.label: Your nerves fray on the spot; the pressure undoes you
You feel the strain of it hard
You are rattled, but still moving
You stay fairly steady through it
Pressure like this barely touches you

questions.text: It is the night before a daunting trial whose outcome you cannot be sure of.

options.label: You lie awake running through every way it could go wrong
You worry over it well into the night
It crosses your mind, then you fall asleep
You give it little thought
You sleep just fine; worrying changes nothing

questions.text: One of those days arrives with a small triumph and a small catastrophe around every corner.

options.label: Your mood swings from giddy to crushed and back again
Your mood shifts often through the day
You have your ups and downs
You stay mostly even
Your mood holds steady from morning to night

questions.text: You go about the ordinary grind of school life, the daily pile of lessons and little demands.

options.label: You are at ease most days, whatever comes
You are generally relaxed
Calm on some days, tense on others
You are often on edge
You are rarely relaxed at all

questions.text: A long, grey stretch of term settles in, the kind of slump where nothing much goes right.

options.label: Low spirits seldom find you; you keep your humour
You rarely sink into the gloom
You have your darker stretches now and then
The blues visit you often
The gloom rarely lifts from you at all
